_comment: Recorded crash dispositions — the durable answer to 'we already diagnosed this; stop logging it as cause: unclear'.
crash_audit.py loads this and, when a recorded disposition matches the leading bugcheck's fault class on this host,
surfaces it in the audit's `disposition` block so the next run/audit reports the KNOWN cause + remediation status
instead of re-deriving an unexplained one. Adding an entry here is how a forensic conclusion becomes referenceable
by tooling (and by GitHub issues like #75 / #111) rather than living only in a prose doc.
Match is intentionally coarse (fault_class, not exact params): a recurring host-level fault recurs with new
params/timestamps but the same class. Tighten `match` with optional board/cpu/dimm fields if a host needs it.
Set `recurring` true for an accepted-recurring host fault so a repeat does not re-open as a fresh unknown.

dispositions.title: PFN_LIST_CORRUPT (0x4E) host memory-subsystem fault
dispositions.summary: Windows kernel bugcheck 0x0000004E (PFN_LIST_CORRUPT) is a HOST memory-subsystem fault, not a fak/fleet code bug (zero `pfn` in the Go source). Diagnosed at high confidence as marginal high-capacity non-ECC DDR5 on a 4-DIMM dual-rank AM5 config; a buggy kernel-mode driver yields identical bugchecks and is only excludable by reading the minidump stack (the named blind spot).
dispositions.remediation: Hardware: MemTest86 + BIOS/AGESA update + (if persists) SoC/VDDG/VDDP nudge or drop to one DIMM pair — needs a drained maintenance window (CRASH-AUDIT §5.A). Fleet: frequent small commits + cold-boot watchdog resume already bound the per-crash loss (§5.B).

observability_blind_spot.signature: ~2 min of total silence (zero warning/error events) before the bugcheck — the signature of a hardware/kernel-memory fault, which by nature cannot pre-announce itself in the software event stream.
observability_blind_spot.accepted_reason: A 0x4E corrupts kernel structures with no graceful unwind, so there is no software trail to emit before the stop. This is an inherent limitation of the fault class, not a missing log.
observability_blind_spot.closure: Switch dump mode from small (CrashControl=3) to kernel (CrashControl=2) so the NEXT bugcheck leaves an attributable minidump stack; the fleet-resilience layer (frequent commits + OS-level cold-boot watchdog resume) is the standing mitigation for the silence itself.
